strategy_justification: Direct HTTP requests time out completely. Standard Playwright browser is blocked by fingerprint-level anti-bot detection (likely Cloudflare or similar WAF). Only UC Chrome (undetected browser) successfully loads the page with full content (~600K chars). Confirmed by site_analysis, product_analysis, and live probe_page — all three converged on uc_chrome_none.
proxy_justification: UC Chrome bypasses anti-bot detection without any proxy. Neither datacenter nor residential proxy is needed. The detection is at the browser fingerprint level, not IP level.
currency_justification: calvinklein.co.uk serves the UK market. GBP is confirmed by .co.uk TLD. Site_analysis incorrectly stated EUR — corrected by product_analysis.

title.js_extraction: const ld = JSON.parse(document.querySelectorAll('script[type="application/ld+json"]')[0].textContent); const items = Array.isArray(ld) ? ld : [ld]; const product = items.find(d => d['@type'] === 'Product'); product?.name

sku.js_extraction: const ld = JSON.parse(document.querySelectorAll('script[type="application/ld+json"]')[0].textContent); const items = Array.isArray(ld) ? ld : [ld]; const product = items.find(d => d['@type'] === 'Product'); product?.sku

brand.js_extraction: const ld = JSON.parse(document.querySelectorAll('script[type="application/ld+json"]')[0].textContent); const items = Array.isArray(ld) ? ld : [ld]; const product = items.find(d => d['@type'] === 'Product'); product?.brand?.name

description.js_extraction: const ld = JSON.parse(document.querySelectorAll('script[type="application/ld+json"]')[0].textContent); const items = Array.isArray(ld) ? ld : [ld]; const product = items.find(d => d['@type'] === 'Product'); product?.description

image.js_extraction: const ld = JSON.parse(document.querySelectorAll('script[type="application/ld+json"]')[0].textContent); const items = Array.isArray(ld) ? ld : [ld]; const product = items.find(d => d['@type'] === 'Product'); product?.image
image.note: May be string or array. If array, take first element.

color.js_extraction: const ld = JSON.parse(document.querySelectorAll('script[type="application/ld+json"]')[0].textContent); const items = Array.isArray(ld) ? ld : [ld]; const product = items.find(d => d['@type'] === 'Product'); product?.color

mpn.js_extraction: const ld = JSON.parse(document.querySelectorAll('script[type="application/ld+json"]')[0].textContent); const items = Array.isArray(ld) ? ld : [ld]; const product = items.find(d => d['@type'] === 'Product'); product?.mpn

price.js_extraction: const prices = []; document.querySelectorAll('span, div, p').forEach(el => { const text = el.textContent.trim(); const match = text.match(/£\s*([0-9]+[.,]?[0-9]{0,2})/); if (match) prices.push({ price: match[0], context: text.substring(0, 80), tag: el.tagName, className: el.className?.substring(0, 60) }); }); prices.filter(p => !/shipp|deliver|return|footer|gift/i.test(p.context)).slice(0, 5)
price.note: JSON-LD offers is EMPTY — price must come from rendered DOM. Page uses hashed CSS classes so exact selectors unknown. Pattern-based search for £ symbol is the best fallback. Filter out non-product prices (shipping, delivery, footer). This selector MUST be tested in scraper and refined based on actual results.

original_price.js_extraction: const origPrices = []; document.querySelectorAll('s, del, [class*="compare"], [class*="was"], [class*="original"], [class*="strike"]').forEach(el => { const text = el.textContent.trim(); const match = text.match(/£\s*([0-9]+[.,]?[0-9]{0,2})/); if (match) origPrices.push({ price: match[0], context: text.substring(0, 80), tag: el.tagName }); }); origPrices
original_price.note: May not exist if product is not on sale. Look for strikethrough elements or 'was' pricing. Untested.

availability.js_extraction: const text = document.body.innerText; if (/out\s+of\s+stock/i.test(text)) return 'Out of Stock'; if (/in\s+stock/i.test(text)) return 'In Stock'; const addBtn = document.querySelector('button[class*="add"], button[class*="cart"], button[class*="bag"]'); if (addBtn && (addBtn.disabled || addBtn.getAttribute('aria-disabled') === 'true')) return 'Out of Stock'; return 'Unknown'
availability.note: Not available in JSON-LD offers. Must extract from rendered DOM text. Check for 'Out of Stock'/'In Stock' text or disabled Add to Bag button. Untested.

currency.note: Inferred from .co.uk TLD. Also extractable from price prefix (£).

url.note: Normalize double-slash to single-slash if present in input URL.

dom_extraction_notes: The page uses a headless React/Next.js frontend backed by Salesforce Commerce Cloud. All CSS classes are hashed/minified (e.g. _abc123_def). No human-readable class names exist. Price extraction relies on text pattern matching (£ followed by digits) rather than CSS selectors. The scraper should extract ALL £-matching elements and filter intelligently (exclude shipping/delivery/footer context). On first run, log all matched price elements for debugging and selector refinement.
variant_notes: Color and size variants are expected for apparel products. Variant data lives in the rendered DOM. The scraper should look for swatch buttons/inputs with data-value or aria-label attributes. Price changes per variant are unknown — verify during testing.

rate_limiting.justification: Medium-severity anti-bot fingerprinting. Use 3-5 second delays between page loads.

cookie_consent_handling: Dismiss cookie consent banner if present. Look for common selectors: button[class*='cookie'], button[class*='consent'], button[class*='accept'], [aria-label*='cookie'], [aria-label*='consent']. Use try/catch to handle absence gracefully.
page_load_wait: Wait for page to fully render before extraction. The page is ~600K chars after JS execution. Use wait_for_selector or wait_for_load_state('networkidle') with a reasonable timeout (15-20 seconds).
confidence_notes: High confidence on strategy (seleniumbase_uc, no proxy) — confirmed by 3 independent analyses. High confidence on JSON-LD fields (7 fields verified). Medium confidence on DOM extraction selectors (price, availability, variants) — pattern-based fallbacks are robust but untested against live DOM. These must be validated during scraper testing and refined based on actual output.
